strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of North Texas (UNT) is a massive, Tier 1 public research university located in Denton, Texas. Known for its world-class College of Music and strong social mobility impact, UNT serves a highly diverse, heavily commuter-based student body. The institution balances accessible assured admissions with rigorous, impacted programs in fields like Computer Science and Music.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic Review for applicants not meeting Assured Admission criteria

analyst_takeaways.insight: Recognized globally for its College of Music, featuring the nation's first jazz studies program, and highly regarded logistics and supply chain programs.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Excellent destination for fine arts and business students seeking high value.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Acceptance rate has dropped from ~84% to 72.2% over five years as applications surged from 21k to over 38k.
analyst_takeaways.implication: While accessible, the university is becoming increasingly competitive, relying heavily on a massive waitlist system to manage yield.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Unpretentious and diverse, with 42% first-generation students, but primarily a commuter campus.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students must actively engage in the 300+ clubs to build community; parking is a common pain point.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Diverse, laid-back, and unpretentious environment with over 300 student organizations. Strong arts and music culture.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Heavily commuter-focused campus; students frequently cite parking availability as a significant daily challenge.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Carnegie R1 institution providing significant undergraduate research opportunities, particularly in sciences and engineering, supported by strong regional industry ties.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test optional. Assured admission is granted based on class rank percentiles and corresponding minimum SAT/ACT scores. Scores are required if submitting for assured admission outside the top 10%.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Application and admit numbers derived from historical enrollment trend data.
Waitlist data sourced from 2024-2025 Common Data Set Section C2.

gpa.notes: UNT does not officially publish average GPA in the CDS (reports 0.00). Estimates place average unweighted GPA at 3.46.

testing.policy_note: Test optional. Approximately 43% submit SAT and 10% submit ACT.

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking high-value STEM education in the DFW metroplex.
colleges.housing_profile: Often houses students at Discovery Park (off main campus).

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.notes: Program is highly impacted; transferring internally can be competitive.

colleges.name: College of Music
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Designed for elite musicians, particularly those interested in jazz, performance, or music education.

majors.name: Music
majors.notes: One of the most competitive music programs in the nation.

colleges.name: G. Brint Ryan College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Excellent for students seeking corporate placements in the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex.

majors.name: General Business
majors.notes: Highly popular major feeding into Dallas-area Fortune 500 companies.

colleges.name: College of Liberal Arts and Social Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Great for humanities and social science students.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: Produces the highest number of graduates per the College Scorecard.

colleges.name: College of Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Suited for pre-med and core science researchers.

application_deadlines.notes: March 1 is the priority deadline for admission and UNT Excellence Scholarship consideration. Rolling admission continues thereafter.

supplemental_requirements.details: Required/Highly Recommended for students who do not meet assured admission standards to trigger a holistic review.
Live or recorded audition required for music programs.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Automatic admission guarantees entry for top 10% or top 25% with qualifying test scores. Holistic review heavily relies on essays to explain academic discrepancies.

major_selection_tactics: Avoid applying to recently phased-out majors like Linguistics or specific ethnic studies minors.
Apply early for impacted programs like Computer Science or Music.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Students failing to gain entry to impacted CS or Engineering programs may start undeclared in the College of Liberal Arts and attempt an internal transfer after fulfilling baseline requirements.

financials.tuition_model: Save and Soar Tuition Plan available to lock in rates.

scholarships.name: National Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Full ride covering tuition, fees, housing, meal plan, books, and stipend for Texas residents ($177k for out of state).

scholarships.name: UNT Excellence Scholarships
scholarships.benefits: Tiered merit awards based on high school rank and GPA.

scholarships.name: Terry Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Comprehensive scholarship for Texas residents demonstrating leadership and need.

ap_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for scores of 3 or higher on most AP exams.
ap_policy.usage: Credits can satisfy core curriculum and specific major prerequisites.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer credits must map to degree applicability; max credit transfers exist for last 30 hours in residency.

what_it_takes: Top 25% class rank combined with a 950+ SAT guarantees entry.
Top 50% class rank requires a 1050+ SAT.
Those applying holistically need a compelling essay emphasizing personal growth.
essay_tips: Address academic blips directly in the Statement of Purpose.
Tie specific UNT resources to your career goals.
red_flags: Low GPA without an explanatory essay.
insights: UNT is known for its welcoming, diverse, and artsy vibe.
Parking is notoriously difficult for commuters.
Discovery Park (Engineering) is off the main campus, requiring a bus ride.
